summary: Matched the lbAnim translation unit by moving the 0x8001E8F8 animation-frame helper out of lbmthp and into lbanim, implementing the previously stubbed lbAnim functions around FigaTree/FigaTrack to HSD_AObj/HSD_FObj setup, updating symbols/splits to extend lbanim through 0x8001E910, and changing fighter call sites to include lbanim.h and call lbAnim_8001E8F8.
what_changed: Marked melee/lb/lbanim.c as Matching in configure.py.
Adjusted GALE01 splits so lbanim.c text now spans 0x8001E560-0x8001E910 and lbmthp.c begins at 0x8001E910 instead of 0x8001E8F8.
Renamed symbol lbAnim_8001E560 to lbAnim_InitFrames and reclassified the function at 0x8001E8F8 from lbMthp_8001E8F8 to lbAnim_8001E8F8.
Implemented fn_8001E60C in lbanim.c to build a linked HSD_FObj list from FigaTrack entries while skipping object types 5, 6, and 7 according to the observed condition.
Changed lbAnim_JObjSortAnim from a static function to a static inline helper, preserving its role as a duplicate of JObjSortAnim.
Completed lbAnim_8001E6D8 with typed HSD_AObj/FigaTree/FigaTrack arguments, HSD_AObj allocation/removal, FObj attachment, sorting, and JOBJ_CLASSICAL_SCALE flag set/clear based on tree->type.
Implemented lbAnim_8001E7E8 as the sibling path that uses fn_8001E60C rather than lbAnim_InitFrames before sorting and applying JOBJ_CLASSICAL_SCALE.
Moved the simple FigaTree frame-count helper from lbmthp.c to lbanim.c as lbAnim_8001E8F8, returning tree->frames or 0.0F when passed NULL.
Updated lbanim.h prototypes from UNK_T placeholders to typed HSD_FObj*, HSD_JObj*, FigaTree*, FigaTrack*, and float declarations.
Removed lbMthp_8001E8F8 declarations from lbmthp.h, lbmthp.dox, and lb/forward.h.
Added a forward declaration for HSD_FObj in src/sysdolphin/baselib/forward.h.
Updated fighter and character initialization call sites in fighter.c, ftCLink, ftDonkey, ftKirby, ftLink, and ftPeach to include lb/lbanim.h and call lbAnim_8001E8F8 instead of lbMthp_8001E8F8.
smart_moves: Recognized that the 0x8001E8F8 frame-count function belonged to the preceding lbanim object, not lbmthp, and fixed both split boundaries and symbol ownership together.
Propagated the module ownership correction through all fighter call sites instead of leaving a misleading lbMthp API wrapper.
Replaced magic flag value 8 with the named JOBJ_CLASSICAL_SCALE flag, improving readability while matching behavior.
Used PAD_STACK(8) where the old local unused array likely only existed to force stack layout, preserving matching intent without keeping misleading variables.
Typed lbanim.h prototypes around FigaTree, FigaTrack, HSD_JObj, and HSD_FObj, reducing UNK_T usage after matching clarified the interfaces.
decomp_lessons: When a function at a file boundary semantically belongs to the previous module, update splits.txt, symbols.txt, headers, documentation stubs, and all call sites in the same PR to avoid fossilizing incorrect ownership.
A tiny helper returning a field such as FigaTree->frames can still be a boundary-correction signal when its address is adjacent to another module and its callers are animation-related.
Matching lb animation setup benefited from modeling the real HSD object graph: FigaTrack data is converted to linked HSD_FObj nodes, attached to an HSD_AObj, then sorted and installed on an HSD_JObj.
Prefer named HSD/JObj flags once the meaning is known; this reduces future churn and helps reviewers distinguish behavior from matching-only artifacts.
Use explicit NULL and 0.0F return paths in small float helpers when the original behavior is a null-safe animation-length lookup.
naming_conventions: Use lbAnim_* for animation-library functions, including boundary helper lbAnim_8001E8F8, rather than leaving animation helpers under lbMthp_*.
Promote a known internal helper name when evidence supports it: lbAnim_8001E560 became lbAnim_InitFrames.
Keep unknown function names as fn_<address> when semantics are not yet named, as with fn_8001E60C.
Use HSD type names directly in prototypes once known: HSD_FObj*, HSD_JObj*, FigaTree*, and FigaTrack*.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Expanded the lbanim split by 0x18 bytes to include the function at 0x8001E8F8, allowing lbanim.c to become Matching.
Used static inline on lbAnim_JObjSortAnim, likely to reproduce expected codegen while keeping it local.
Replaced an unused stack array with PAD_STACK(8), a clearer matching tactic for stack-shape preservation.
Used float constants with F suffix, such as 0.0F, in HSD_AObjSetRewindFrame and null-return paths.
The fn_8001E60C implementation maintains first/prev pointers and explicitly terminates the linked HSD_FObj list with fobj->next = NULL, matching linked-list construction patterns.
review_feedback: No review comments were included in the provided PR slice. The PR had one issue comment according to counts, but its body was not present in the excerpt, so no reviewer intent can be extracted.
follow_up_queries: Search for remaining lbMthp_8001E8F8 references to ensure the boundary correction was fully propagated.
Investigate fn_8001E60C semantics and whether it can be named after its FigaTrack filtering behavior.
Compare lbAnim_JObjSortAnim against baselib JObjSortAnim to confirm whether deduplication or a shared helper is possible later.
Review neighboring lbmthp functions starting at 0x8001E910 now that the split boundary was corrected.
